gimp-channel-combine-masks.help: This procedure combines two channel masks. The result is stored in the first channel.

args.name: operation
args.desc: The selection operation { CHANNEL-OP-ADD (0), CHANNEL-OP-SUBTRACT (1), CHANNEL-OP-REPLACE (2), CHANNEL-OP-INTERSECT (3) }

args.name: offx
args.desc: x offset between upper left corner of channels: (second - first)

args.name: offy
args.desc: y offset between upper left corner of channels: (second - first)

gimp-channel-copy.help:
This procedure copies the specified channel and returns the copy.
The new channel still needs to be added to the image, as this is not automatic. Add the new channel with 'gimp-image-insert-channel'.

gimp-channel-delete.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-delete' instead.
gimp-channel-delete.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-delete' instead.

gimp-channel-get-color.blurb: Get the compositing color of the specified channel.
gimp-channel-get-color.help: This procedure returns the specified channel's compositing color.

gimp-channel-get-name.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-name' instead.
gimp-channel-get-name.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-name' instead.

gimp-channel-get-opacity.blurb: Get the opacity of the specified channel.
gimp-channel-get-opacity.help: This procedure returns the specified channel's opacity.

gimp-channel-get-show-masked.blurb: Get the composite method of the specified channel.
gimp-channel-get-show-masked.help: This procedure returns the specified channel's composite method. If it is TRUE, then the channel is composited with the image so that masked regions are shown. Otherwise, selected regions are shown.

gimp-channel-get-tattoo.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-tattoo' instead.
gimp-channel-get-tattoo.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-tattoo' instead.

gimp-channel-get-visible.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-visible' instead.
gimp-channel-get-visible.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-visible' instead.

gimp-channel-new.help:
This procedure creates a new channel with the specified width, height, name, opacity and color.
The new channel still needs to be added to the image, as this is not automatic. Add the new channel with 'gimp-image-insert-channel'. Other attributes, such as channel visibility, should be set with explicit procedure calls.
The channel's contents are undefined initially.

args.name: image
args.desc: The image to which to add the channel

gimp-channel-new-from-component.blurb: Create a new channel from a color component
gimp-channel-new-from-component.help:
This procedure creates a new channel from a color component.
The new channel still needs to be added to the image, as this is not automatic. Add the new channel with 'gimp-image-insert-channel'. Other attributes, such as channel visibility, should be set with explicit procedure calls.

args.name: image
args.desc: The image to which to add the channel

args.name: component
args.desc: The image component { CHANNEL-RED (0), CHANNEL-GREEN (1), CHANNEL-BLUE (2), CHANNEL-GRAY (3), CHANNEL-INDEXED (4), CHANNEL-ALPHA (5) }

gimp-channel-ops-duplicate.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-duplicate' instead.
gimp-channel-ops-duplicate.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-duplicate' instead.

gimp-channel-ops-offset.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-drawable-offset' instead.
gimp-channel-ops-offset.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-drawable-offset' instead.

args.name: wrap-around
args.desc: wrap image around or fill vacated regions (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: fill-type
args.desc: fill vacated regions of drawable with background or transparent { OFFSET-BACKGROUND (0), OFFSET-TRANSPARENT (1), OFFSET-WRAP-AROUND (2) }

args.name: offset-x
args.desc: offset by this amount in X direction

args.name: offset-y
args.desc: offset by this amount in Y direction

gimp-channel-set-color.blurb: Set the compositing color of the specified channel.
gimp-channel-set-color.help: This procedure sets the specified channel's compositing color.

gimp-channel-set-name.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-set-name' instead.
gimp-channel-set-name.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-set-name' instead.

gimp-channel-set-opacity.blurb: Set the opacity of the specified channel.
gimp-channel-set-opacity.help: This procedure sets the specified channel's opacity.

gimp-channel-set-show-masked.blurb: Set the composite method of the specified channel.
gimp-channel-set-show-masked.help: This procedure sets the specified channel's composite method. If it is TRUE, then the channel is composited with the image so that masked regions are shown. Otherwise, selected regions are shown.

args.name: show-masked
args.desc: The new channel composite method (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-channel-set-tattoo.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-set-tattoo' instead.
gimp-channel-set-tattoo.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-set-tattoo' instead.

gimp-channel-set-visible.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-set-visible' instead.
gimp-channel-set-visible.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-set-visible' instead.

args.name: visible
args.desc: The new item visibility (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-image-select-color.blurb: Create a selection by selecting all pixels (in the specified drawable) with the same (or similar) color to that specified.
gimp-image-select-color.help:
This tool creates a selection over the specified image. A by-color selection is determined by the supplied color under the constraints of the current context settings. Essentially, all pixels (in the drawable) that have color sufficiently close to the specified color (as determined by the threshold and criterion context values) are included in the selection. To select transparent regions, the color specified must also have minimum alpha.

This procedure is affected by the following context setters: 'gimp-context-set-antialias', 'gimp-context-set-feather', 'gimp-context-set-feather-radius', 'gimp-context-set-sample-merged', 'gimp-context-set-sample-criterion', 'gimp-context-set-sample-threshold', 'gimp-context-set-sample-transparent'.

In the case of a merged sampling, the supplied drawable is ignored.

args.name: operation
args.desc: The selection operation { CHANNEL-OP-ADD (0), CHANNEL-OP-SUBTRACT (1), CHANNEL-OP-REPLACE (2), CHANNEL-OP-INTERSECT (3) }

gimp-image-select-contiguous-color.blurb: Create a selection by selecting all pixels around specified coordinates with the same (or similar) color to that at the coordinates.
gimp-image-select-contiguous-color.help:
This tool creates a contiguous selection over the specified image. A contiguous color selection is determined by a seed fill under the constraints of the current context settings. Essentially, the color at the specified coordinates (in the drawable) is measured and the selection expands outwards from that point to any adjacent pixels which are not significantly different (as determined by the threshold and criterion context settings). This process continues until no more expansion is possible. If antialiasing is turned on, the final selection mask will contain intermediate values based on close misses to the threshold bar at pixels along the seed fill boundary.

This procedure is affected by the following context setters: 'gimp-context-set-antialias', 'gimp-context-set-feather', 'gimp-context-set-feather-radius', 'gimp-context-set-sample-merged', 'gimp-context-set-sample-criterion', 'gimp-context-set-sample-threshold', 'gimp-context-set-sample-transparent', 'gimp-context-set-diagonal-neighbors'.

In the case of a merged sampling, the supplied drawable is ignored. If the sample is merged, the specified coordinates are relative to the image origin; otherwise, they are relative to the drawable's origin.

args.name: operation
args.desc: The selection operation { CHANNEL-OP-ADD (0), CHANNEL-OP-SUBTRACT (1), CHANNEL-OP-REPLACE (2), CHANNEL-OP-INTERSECT (3) }

args.name: x
args.desc: x coordinate of initial seed fill point: (image coordinates)

args.name: y
args.desc: y coordinate of initial seed fill point: (image coordinates)

gimp-image-select-ellipse.blurb: Create an elliptical selection over the specified image.
gimp-image-select-ellipse.help:
This tool creates an elliptical selection over the specified image. The elliptical region can be either added to, subtracted from, or replace the contents of the previous selection mask.

This procedure is affected by the following context setters: 'gimp-context-set-antialias', 'gimp-context-set-feather', 'gimp-context-set-feather-radius'.

args.name: operation
args.desc: The selection operation { CHANNEL-OP-ADD (0), CHANNEL-OP-SUBTRACT (1), CHANNEL-OP-REPLACE (2), CHANNEL-OP-INTERSECT (3) }

args.name: x
args.desc: x coordinate of upper-left corner of ellipse bounding box

args.name: y
args.desc: y coordinate of upper-left corner of ellipse bounding box

gimp-image-select-item.blurb: Transforms the specified item into a selection
gimp-image-select-item.help:
This procedure renders the item's outline into the current selection of the image the item belongs to. What exactly the item's outline is depends on the item type: for layers, it's the layer's alpha channel, for vectors the vector's shape.

This procedure is affected by the following context setters: 'gimp-context-set-antialias', 'gimp-context-set-feather', 'gimp-context-set-feather-radius'.

args.name: operation
args.desc: The desired operation with current selection { CHANNEL-OP-ADD (0), CHANNEL-OP-SUBTRACT (1), CHANNEL-OP-REPLACE (2), CHANNEL-OP-INTERSECT (3) }

args.name: item
args.desc: The item to render to the selection

gimp-image-select-polygon.blurb: Create a polygonal selection over the specified image.
gimp-image-select-polygon.help:
This tool creates a polygonal selection over the specified image. The polygonal region can be either added to, subtracted from, or replace the contents of the previous selection mask. The polygon is specified through an array of floating point numbers and its length. The length of array must be 2n, where n is the number of points. Each point is defined by 2 floating point values which correspond to the x and y coordinates. If the final point does not connect to the starting point, a connecting segment is automatically added.

This procedure is affected by the following context setters: 'gimp-context-set-antialias', 'gimp-context-set-feather', 'gimp-context-set-feather-radius'.

args.name: operation
args.desc: The selection operation { CHANNEL-OP-ADD (0), CHANNEL-OP-SUBTRACT (1), CHANNEL-OP-REPLACE (2), CHANNEL-OP-INTERSECT (3) }

args.name: num-segs
args.desc: Number of points (count 1 coordinate as two points) (num-segs >= 2)

args.name: segs
args.desc: Array of points: { p1.x, p1.y, p2.x, p2.y, ..., pn.x, pn.y}

gimp-image-select-rectangle.blurb: Create a rectangular selection over the specified image;
gimp-image-select-rectangle.help:
This tool creates a rectangular selection over the specified image. The rectangular region can be either added to, subtracted from, or replace the contents of the previous selection mask.

This procedure is affected by the following context setters: 'gimp-context-set-feather', 'gimp-context-set-feather-radius'.

args.name: operation
args.desc: The selection operation { CHANNEL-OP-ADD (0), CHANNEL-OP-SUBTRACT (1), CHANNEL-OP-REPLACE (2), CHANNEL-OP-INTERSECT (3) }

args.name: x
args.desc: x coordinate of upper-left corner of rectangle

args.name: y
args.desc: y coordinate of upper-left corner of rectangle

gimp-image-select-round-rectangle.blurb: Create a rectangular selection with round corners over the specified image;
gimp-image-select-round-rectangle.help:
This tool creates a rectangular selection with round corners over the specified image. The rectangular region can be either added to, subtracted from, or replace the contents of the previous selection mask.

This procedure is affected by the following context setters: 'gimp-context-set-antialias', 'gimp-context-set-feather', 'gimp-context-set-feather-radius'.

args.name: operation
args.desc: The selection operation { CHANNEL-OP-ADD (0), CHANNEL-OP-SUBTRACT (1), CHANNEL-OP-REPLACE (2), CHANNEL-OP-INTERSECT (3) }

args.name: x
args.desc: x coordinate of upper-left corner of rectangle

args.name: y
args.desc: y coordinate of upper-left corner of rectangle

args.name: corner-radius-x
args.desc: The corner radius in X direction (0 <= corner-radius-x <= 524288)

args.name: corner-radius-y
args.desc: The corner radius in Y direction (0 <= corner-radius-y <= 524288)

gimp-selection-all.help: This procedure sets the selection mask to completely encompass the image. Every pixel in the selection channel is set to 255.

gimp-selection-border.help: This procedure borders the selection. Bordering creates a new selection which is defined along the boundary of the previous selection at every point within the specified radius.

gimp-selection-bounds.blurb: Find the bounding box of the current selection.
gimp-selection-bounds.help: This procedure returns whether there is a selection for the specified image. If there is one, the upper left and lower right corners of the bounding box are returned. These coordinates are relative to the image. Please note that the pixel specified by the lower right coordinate of the bounding box is not part of the selection. The selection ends at the upper left corner of this pixel. This means the width of the selection can be calculated as (x2 - x1), its height as (y2 - y1).

gimp-selection-clear.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-selection-none' instead.
gimp-selection-clear.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-selection-none' instead.

args.name: operation
args.desc: The selection operation { CHANNEL-OP-ADD (0), CHANNEL-OP-SUBTRACT (1), CHANNEL-OP-REPLACE (2), CHANNEL-OP-INTERSECT (3) }

gimp-selection-feather.help: This procedure feathers the selection. Feathering is implemented using a gaussian blur.

gimp-selection-float.blurb: Float the selection from the specified drawable with initial offsets as specified.
gimp-selection-float.help: This procedure determines the region of the specified drawable that lies beneath the current selection. The region is then cut from the drawable and the resulting data is made into a new layer which is instantiated as a floating selection. The offsets allow initial positioning of the new floating selection.

args.name: drawable
args.desc: The drawable from which to float selection

gimp-selection-flood.blurb: Remove holes from the image's selection
gimp-selection-flood.help: This procedure removes holes from the selection, that can come from selecting a patchy area with the Fuzzy Select Tool. In technical terms this procedure floods the selection. See the Algorithms page in the developer wiki for details.

gimp-selection-grow.help: This procedure grows the selection. Growing involves expanding the boundary in all directions by the specified pixel amount.

gimp-selection-invert.help: This procedure inverts the selection mask. For every pixel in the selection channel, its new value is calculated as (255 - old-value).

gimp-selection-is-empty.blurb: Determine whether the selection is empty.
gimp-selection-is-empty.help: This procedure returns TRUE if the selection for the specified image is empty.

gimp-selection-none.help: This procedure deselects the entire image. Every pixel in the selection channel is set to 0.

gimp-selection-save.blurb: Copy the selection mask to a new channel.
gimp-selection-save.help: This procedure copies the selection mask and stores the content in a new channel. The new channel is automatically inserted into the image's list of channels.

gimp-selection-sharpen.help: This procedure sharpens the selection mask. For every pixel in the selection channel, if the value is > 127, the new pixel is assigned a value of 255. This removes any "anti-aliasing" that might exist in the selection mask's boundary.

gimp-selection-shrink.help: This procedure shrinks the selection. Shrinking involves trimming the existing selection boundary on all sides by the specified number of pixels.

gimp-selection-translate.blurb: Translate the selection by the specified offsets.
gimp-selection-translate.help: This procedure actually translates the selection for the specified image by the specified offsets. Regions that are translated from beyond the bounds of the image are set to empty. Valid regions of the selection which are translated beyond the bounds of the image because of this call are lost.

gimp-selection-value.blurb: Find the value of the selection at the specified coordinates.
gimp-selection-value.help: This procedure returns the value of the selection at the specified coordinates. If the coordinates lie out of bounds, 0 is returned.
